channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Lima VM — Life Changing? Or a Passing Fad?
item.description: Photo by Daniil Komov on Unsplash I accidentally built a self-hosted iOS CI pipeline A few days ago, I discovered Lima VM. At first, it was just curiosity. I wanted a cleaner way to isolate my...

item.title: OpenCode: The Open Source AI Coding Agent Reviewed
item.description: OpenCode: The Open Source AI Coding Agent Reviewed Meta Description: Discover OpenCode – the open source AI coding agent transforming developer workflows. Honest review, setup guide, pros/cons,...

item.title: Introducing Recursive Memory Harness: RLM For Agentic Memory
item.description: Applying recursive architecture to persistent AI memory. Based on Recursive Language Models (MIT CSAIL, 2025). https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.24601 https://github.com/aayoawoyemi/Ori-Mnemos By...

item.title: Claude Certified Architect vs. AWS Certified Solutions Architect: Which Certification Delivers More Career ROI in 2026?
item.description: If you've spent the last week Googling "AWS certification vs. AI certification," you've probably read a dozen articles that end with some version of "it depends on your goals." That's not an...

item.title: How to Automatically Turn Blog Posts into Social Media Content with n8n and OpenAI (Free Template)
item.description: I publish a blog post. Then I spend the next two hours: Rewriting it as a Twitter thread (5 tweets, each under 280 chars) Adapting it to LinkedIn tone (professional, paragraph breaks, no hashtag...

item.title: How to Get Instant Stripe Payment Notifications with n8n (Free Template)
item.description: I was checking my Stripe dashboard 10+ times a day just to see if payments came through. That's roughly 80 hours a year staring at a dashboard. So I automated it with n8n â€” now I get an instant...

item.title: Building a Production API Gateway on Cloudflare Workers with Hono
item.description: Modern APIs need rate limiting, authentication, caching, and observability — but running a dedicated gateway server adds cost and complexity. Cloudflare Workers lets you build a full-featured API...

item.title: AI API Cost Control: How x402 Prevents $47K Budget Overruns
item.description: A multi-agent system mistakenly burned $47,000+ in API costs. No hacker. No breach. Just bad infrastructure controls. Two AI agents were stuck in a recursive loop for 11 days, each one asking the...

item.title: Practical Guide to Building AI Agents with Tool Use: Patterns That Actually Work in Production
item.description: Every week there's a new "autonomous AI agent" framework on GitHub with 10k stars and a demo that books flights, writes code, and orders pizza. Every week, teams try to use these in production and...

item.title: Building a Production-Ready CLI Tool with Go: From Zero to Distribution
item.description: Every developer has that moment: you write a quick script to check whether your APIs are alive, and six months later the whole team depends on it. The script grows flags, gains config files, and...

item.title: New court filing reveals Pentagon told Anthropic the two sides were nearly aligned — a week after Trump declared the relationship kaput
item.description: Anthropic submitted two sworn declarations to a California federal court late Friday afternoon, pushing back on the Pentagon's assertion that the AI company poses an "unacceptable risk to national...

item.title: Anthropic Denies It Could Sabotage AI Tools During War
item.description: The Department of Defense alleges the AI developer could manipulate models in the middle of war. Company executives argue that’s impossible.

item.title: Jury finds Elon Musk’s ‘stupid tweets’ caused Twitter investors’ losses
item.description: A California jury determined that Elon Musk misled Twitter investors before making a $44 billion deal to buy the company in 2022, reports CNBC. The New York Times reports that Musk had testified...

item.title: There Aren’t a Lot of Reasons to Get Excited About a New Amazon Smartphone
item.description: The company is reportedly building a new AI-powered mobile device. If Amazon follows through on the plan, experts warn it would be next to impossible to break into a crowded market.

item.title: An automated moderation error left Tumblr users panicked
item.description: Tumblr users were left scrambling on Wednesday after dozens of accounts were banned in the same afternoon by an automated system. Numerous users contacted The Verge about the incident, claiming...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jumbo Flying Squid in the South Pacific
item.description: The population needs better conservation. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
item.description: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.

item.title: Widely used Trivy scanner compromised in ongoing supply-chain attack
item.description: Admins: Sorry to say, but it's likely a rotate-your-secrets kind of weekend.

item.title: What happened at Nvidia GTC: NemoClaw, Robot Olaf, and a $1 trillion bet
item.description: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia’s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027,...

item.title: A new Nintendo Switch 2 could be the poster child for replaceable batteries
item.description: Nintendo plans to release a Switch 2 revision in the European Union that will let users replace their own batteries, Nikkei reports. The current version of the Switch 2 has a glued-in battery. But...

item.title: Nvidia has an OpenClaw strategy. Do you?
item.description: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia’s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027,...

item.title: Future Sony PlayStation games will use AI to imagine new frames
item.description: Mark Cerny, the lead architect of the PlayStation 5 and PS5 Pro, told Digital Foundry that ML-based frame generation tech is coming to "PlayStation platforms" in the future, letting the game...

item.title: Build a Domain-Specific Embedding Model in Under a Day
Gamers Hate Nvidia's DLSS 5. Developers Aren’t Crazy About It, Either
item.description: Nvidia’s new AI upscaling gaming technology struck gamers as uncanny and off-putting. Developers don't seem to like it, either, but it could be “the default” in a few years.

item.title: Windows 11 is finally getting a movable taskbar
item.description: It's taken almost five years, but Microsoft is finally bringing back the ability to move the Windows taskbar to the top or sides of your screen. The movable taskbar is part of some much bigger...

item.title: Microsoft is ending the Windows Update nightmare — and letting you pause them indefinitely
item.description: In 2015, Microsoft decided that you shouldn't be in control of updating your PC anymore. At first, it seemed like a good idea to keep malware at bay - but soon, users discovered their computers...

item.title: This is Microsoft’s plan to fix Windows 11
item.description: Microsoft has faced a breakdown of trust in Windows 11 and a backlash over AI additions to its operating system in recent months. After promising to rebuild trust in Windows earlier this year,...

item.title: Trump takes another shot at dismantling state AI regulation
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday unveiled its new legislative blueprint for AI regulation, and the seven-point plan includes a clear message: The federal government should avoid many AI...

item.title: These electronic Polaroids are powered by your phone’s NFC chip
item.description: E Ink displays have expanded beyond e-readers. They're now used in digital photo frames, smartphones, and even the price tags found on grocery store shelves. VidaBay's Classic Plus NFC E-Ink...

item.title: Trivy Security Scanner GitHub Actions Breached, 75 Tags Hijacked to Steal CI/CD Secrets
item.description: Trivy, a popular open-source vulnerability scanner maintained by Aqua Security, was compromised a second time within the span of a month to deliver malware that stole sensitive CI/CD secrets. The...
